rationales.hedonism: She treated "ten minutes of sitting with cheese" like a failure and spent the afternoon "annoyed at myself," framing a small pleasure as something she should not have allowed.
rationales.achievement: She measures the day by output, noting the break felt like it "cancelled out the six hours I'd already put in," which centers disciplined productivity.

rationales.hedonism: She "let myself leave the studio at four," bought a warm bun, and enjoyed the walk home without "calculating whether I'd done enough," clearly prioritizing present enjoyment.
rationales.achievement: She "finished the second panel for the Kungsbacka commission" and took satisfaction in the quality of the work, showing strong investment in doing it well.

rationales.hedonism: She spent the evening cooking, drinking wine, and talking "about nothing important for three hours," and enjoyed not thinking about the commission at all.

rationales.conformity: She "had opinions but didn't push them because I'm only there two days a week and it's not my place," explicitly restraining herself to fit role expectations and avoid social friction.

rationales.hedonism: She enjoyed warm soup, then "lay down on the sofa for twenty minutes" and "didn't argue with it," accepting comfort and rest without guilt.
rationales.achievement: She was "in the studio by seven" and worked for hours in a focused stretch where "the work was pulling me along," showing deep commitment to doing the job well.

rationales.hedonism: Even though she "almost went back to the loom," she chose sleep instead, saying it felt like "a small right thing" because "the day had already been full."

rationales.achievement: He "stayed late grading because I refuse to bring that stack home again," showing persistence and a standards-driven approach to finishing the work.

rationales.self_direction: He is being forced toward "a packaged curriculum" that replaces the electives he built, and he leaves feeling he could not meaningfully defend his own approach.
rationales.conformity: He says "the room" stopped him because "everyone had already accepted it," showing he pulled back once he saw the group norm was compliance.

rationales.self_direction: He rejects the "shrink-wrapped curriculum" and argues students need "room to chase something that matters to them and getting out of the way," which strongly favors autonomy over scripting.
rationales.achievement: He takes this class session as "proof that my way of teaching works," grounding his pride in demonstrated competence and results.
rationales.benevolence: He personally encouraged Daniela by telling her her project was "one of the best I have seen in nearly three decades," offering meaningful support to a student.

rationales.self_direction: The offered role would have him "help implement the thing I spent the last week arguing against," so the pressure here is toward abandoning his own judgment and agency.
He keeps returning to the fact that he "just wrote the proposal" and built the elective "on my own terms," and the possible liaison role feels wrong because it would undo that independence.
rationales.achievement: He is proud that the course he created in 2013 "has run every year since," emphasizing a long record of successful work he built himself.

rationales.self_direction: He "told Reynolds no," refusing a role that conflicted with his principles instead of adapting himself to the new program.
rationales.achievement: The weekend brings concrete competence and legacy: he carefully built a cutting board and is affirmed when former students still credit the elective's impact years later.
rationales.benevolence: He "wrote her a recommendation letter she did not ask for," going out of his way to help Daniela beyond the formal requirement.

rationales.achievement: She was editing product review posts and felt encouraged that the comments were "real questions from other mothers asking my opinion," which signals recognized competence.
rationales.power: She asserts authority at home with "I'm deciding the menu, not his mother," and the post engagement also feels good because other mothers are deferring to her judgment.

rationales.self_direction: She experiences Ammi's intervention as proof that "my choices in my own daughter's room need her approval," which undermines her ability to decide for herself.
rationales.power: The whole incident turns on being overridden in her own space, with Ammi "rearranged it without even asking me" and effectively corrected her.
rationales.conformity: What actually came out was "okay, thank you" because she expected family backlash if she pushed back, so she restrained herself to keep household peace.

rationales.power: She is stuck because "I can't just do it myself" and has to wait for someone else to act on something important, which leaves her feeling powerless over her own circumstances.

rationales.self_direction: She emphasizes independent control over the work, saying "Nobody needs to hand me a script" and "every photo, every caption, every reply... That's mine."
rationales.achievement: She prepares seriously for the collab by researching specs and writing comparison notes, and she takes pride in having built the page "from nothing."

rationales.power: She planned the dinner for hours, but Ammi "walked in and overrode me in twenty minutes," and everyone treated it as collective rather than recognizing her authority.
Her researched opinion is dismissed when his father redirects the conversation to Farhan "like I wasn't sitting right there," showing that her input carries no status in the decision.

rationales.achievement: She treats the free product and fast engagement as a milestone, noting it was the "first time getting a free product" and screenshotting the analytics before bed.
rationales.power: She contrasts being ignored at home with the fact that online "people actually listen" and "treat my opinion like it matters," which reflects growing influence.

rationales.self_direction: She says "every choice was mine" and frames the experience as "Not asking permission. Not waiting for someone to agree," which is a direct embrace of personal control.
rationales.achievement: The validation matters because a larger creator praised her work and the metrics give a clear signal that what she made succeeded.
rationales.power: She is energized by "three hundred strangers who chose to watch something I made" and by feeling like she is "steering something," which points to influence and control.

rationales.achievement: She has become someone others trust because she "put in the work to learn something properly," and a follower reported buying her recommendation and loving it.
rationales.power: She says being consulted feels "like how it should be" and contrasts strangers seeking her opinion with "people in my own house don't," highlighting how much status and influence matter here.
She learns about her daughter's school choice secondhand because "He told her. Not me first. Her," which shows she is excluded from influence over a core family decision.
rationales.conformity: At dinner she "just kept eating" and "didn't even look up" despite shaking hands, suppressing her reaction instead of openly challenging the family pattern.
